codeReview.summary: All 5 polish fixes are correctly implemented, well-tested, and follow existing codebase conventions. Each fix targets a specific issue identified in prior scrutiny reviews. The changes are minimal and focused, touching only the necessary files (parse.go, token.go, unmarshal.go, ast_test.go) plus a new polish_test.go with 8 test cases covering all fixes. All assignMap call sites (5 total) were correctly updated to propagate the strict parameter. The Unmarshaler check in assignSliceFromArray correctly prioritizes pointer-receiver checks before value-receiver checks, matching the existing pattern used in assignValue and assignPointerValue.

issues.description: Residual casing inconsistency: error message says 'expected Zulu formatted DateTime' (capital T) while the fix aligned all other references to 'Datetime'. This is a user-facing error string, not API surface, so it's non-blocking but could be cleaned up for full consistency.

summary: All 5 polish fixes pass review: (1) sepKnown=true correctly prevents parent lexer from overwriting included file key separators, (2) DateTime→Datetime casing is now consistent between ItemType.String() and DatetimeNode.Type(), (3) all three enum String() methods use safe fmt.Sprintf fallback instead of panicking, (4) strict mode is properly propagated through all 5 assignMap call sites to unmarshalMap for nested structs in maps, (5) Unmarshaler interface check in assignSliceFromArray correctly handles both pointer and value receiver cases before kind-based branching. Tests are comprehensive with 8 new test cases in polish_test.go plus one updated test in ast_test.go. One non-blocking observation: parse.go:336 still has 'DateTime' casing in an error message string.
